overview:
## Discussion

This error is the same as the AppleScript error [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/kernel/1645157-anonymous/usercancelederr]. When an Apple Event is canceled by the user, a running action may return this error. Automator ignores the error and stops the workflow gracefully, without displaying the error to the user.

# AMError.Code.userCanceledError

**Case**

An error that indicates the user cancelled.

## Declaration

```swift
case userCanceledError
```

## Discussion

This error is the same as the AppleScript error [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/kernel/1645157-anonymous/usercancelederr]. When an Apple Event is canceled by the user, a running action may return this error. Automator ignores the error and stops the workflow gracefully, without displaying the error to the user.
title: AMError.Code.userCanceledError
